title: Start Here — Your FAANG Preparation Curriculum
description: Not sure where to begin? This guide helps you assess your current level and provides a week-by-week curriculum tailored to your experience. Four paths: Junior (0-3 years), Mid (3-7), Senior (7-15), and Staff (15+). Includes a dependency graph showing which topics to read before others.

sections.title: How to Use This Site — The Dependency Graph

description.value:
The ABAP Bytes site has 45+ content files and 8 interactive tools. Without guidance, it is easy to get lost. This section shows you the optimal reading order based on topic dependencies.

Core principle: Content is organized in layers. Each layer assumes you have read the previous layer. The dependency graph is: FAANG Bridge (vocabulary and fundamentals) → ABAP Foundations (core platform knowledge) → Modern ABAP (current SAP architecture) → Integration & Enterprise → Leadership & Interview → System Design → Knowledge Amplifiers (force multipliers). Within each layer, there are recommended reading orders.

Prerequisite rules: (1) Read 'FAANG Mindset Shift' before any other content — it establishes the vocabulary translation layer you need for everything else. (2) Read 'Translation Map' before any system design or interview content — it gives you the DS vocabulary for all topics. (3) Read 'System Design Fundamentals' before any domain-specific system design file (Global ERP, Integration Hub, etc.). (4) Read 'Staff+ Technical Framing' before any interview content — it establishes the 5 Signals framework you need for all answers. (5) Read 'Elephant in the Room' after you have started interview preparation — it is most useful when you understand the interview process.

Using the tools: The tools (Quiz Engine, Mock Simulator, Study Planner, Readiness Assessment) are independent of the content — you can use them at any time. However, for best results: (1) Take the Readiness Assessment first to identify your starting point. (2) Use the Study Planner to schedule your reading based on this guide. (3) Use the Quiz Engine after each content file to test retention. (4) Use the Mock Simulator after you have completed 50%+ of your content reading.

Answer these 5 questions to determine your starting path. Be honest — overestimating your level wastes time, underestimating wastes opportunity.

Question 1: How many years of SAP experience do you have? (A) 0-3 years — Junior Path. (B) 3-7 years — Mid Path. (C) 7-15 years — Senior Path. (D) 15+ years — Staff Path.

Question 2: How would you rate your distributed systems knowledge? (A) I am not confident explaining CAP theorem or consistent hashing — Junior Path. (B) I understand the concepts but cannot design a distributed system from scratch — Mid Path. (C) I can design and explain tradeoffs for a distributed system — Senior Path. (D) I have designed and operated distributed systems at scale — Staff Path.

Question 3: How many LeetCode problems have you solved? (A) 0-50 — Junior Path. (B) 50-150 — Mid Path. (C) 150-300 — Senior Path. (D) 300+ — Staff Path.

Question 4: How many production system design projects have you led? (A) 0 — guided exercises needed — Junior Path. (B) 1-2 with mentorship — Mid Path. (C) 3-5 independently — Senior Path. (D) 5+ with cross-team scope — Staff Path.

Question 5: How would you rate your behavioral storytelling ability? (A) I have not prepared STAR stories — Junior Path. (B) I have 3-4 stories but they need work — Mid Path. (C) I have well-prepared stories with quantified impact — Senior Path. (D) My stories demonstrate Staff+ signals (impact, scope, depth, vision, communication) — Staff Path.

Scoring: If you selected mostly A → Junior Path. Mostly B → Mid Path. Mostly C → Senior Path. Mostly D → Staff Path. If you are between two levels (e.g., experience at C, but LeetCode at B), choose the lower path for safety and supplement with the higher path's system design content.

This path focuses on building fundamentals. As a junior SAP engineer, your FAANG transition requires strengthening three areas: algorithmic coding (which ABAP does not train), distributed systems concepts (which FAANG interviews heavily), and behavioral storytelling (which SAP does not evaluate).

Week 1-2: FAANG Bridge + ABAP Foundations. Read: FAANG Mindset Shift (establish vocabulary), Translation Map (learn the SAP→DS pairs), ABAP Platform Architecture (understand your runtime), ABAP OOP Staff+ Design (design patterns). Goal: You should be able to translate 10 SAP concepts to DS equivalents by end of Week 2.

Week 3-4: LeetCode Fundamentals. Focus on the most common patterns: Arrays and Strings (80% of LeetCode Easy), Hash Maps (key to O(1) lookup problems), Linked Lists (pointer manipulation), Trees and Graphs (DFS/BFS). Solve 50 problems total. Do not skip to DP yet — you need pattern recognition first. Use Python (recommended for FAANG interviews). Goal: Solve any LeetCode Easy within 30 minutes, start Medium.

Week 5-6: System Design Fundamentals. Read: System Design Fundamentals (CAP, consistent hashing, caching, sharding), Design Global ERP Platform (your SAP background helps here — you understand enterprise scale), Design Integration Hub (event-driven architecture). Goal: You can whiteboard a basic system design with a client, API gateway, service layer, database, and cache. You understand CAP tradeoffs.

Week 7-8: LeetCode Intermediate. Continue solving problems — focus on the Medium tier. Add: Dynamic Programming (start with Fibonacci, climb stairs, knapSack), Graphs (BFS/DFS for connected components, shortest path), Stacks and Queues (monotonic stack patterns). Target: 100 total problems solved. Goal: Solve any LeetCode Medium within 45 minutes with hint.

Week 9-10: Behavioral + Interview Prep. Read: Staff+ Technical Framing (5 Signals — prepare 3 stories), Interview Playbook (phone screen, loop strategy), Elephant in the Room (preempt objections). Prepare 3 SAP stories in STAR format. Practice them aloud until you can deliver each in 2-3 minutes. Goal: Every story hits at least 2 of the 5 Staff+ signals.

Week 11-12: Mock Interviews + Final Prep. Take the Readiness Assessment. Do 3 mock interviews (use Mock Simulator tool). Identify weak areas and focus on them. Read: Offer Negotiation (understand comp — motivate yourself). Goal: Ready to apply and interview.

This path builds on your existing SAP depth. You know ABAP and SAP architecture well. Your gaps are: LeetCode fluency (you have likely never solved algorithmic problems), system design depth (you know SAP patterns but not generic DS patterns), and behavioral strategy (you have stories but have not prepared them for FAANG evaluation).

Week 1-2: Vocabulary + LeetCode Launch. Read: Translation Map (critical — learn 15+ pairs in 2 weeks), FAANG Mindset Shift (establish vocabulary). Start LeetCode: solve 30 Easy problems to build confidence. Focus on Python syntax and the core patterns (arrays, strings, hash maps). Goal: By end of Week 2, you can translate any SAP concept to DS equivalents and solve LeetCode Easy without hints.

Week 3-4: LeetCode Intermediate + System Design. Solve 50 Medium problems (total: 80 solved). Focus on: Trees (BFS/DFS, recursion), Graphs (adjacency list, shortest path), Dynamic Programming (patterns: 0/1 knapSack, longest subsequence). In parallel, read: System Design Fundamentals, Design Global ERP Platform, Design Integration Hub. Goal: Solve any LeetCode Medium within 45 minutes with minimal hints. Whiteboard a medium-complexity system design (e.g., design a chat system, design a URL shortener).

Week 5-6: System Design Depth + Behavioral Foundations. Read: DDIA Replication & Partitioning (SAP enqueue maps to Raft consensus), API Design for Platforms (OData → REST/GraphQL), Design Master Data Management (MDM patterns). Prepare 4 STAR stories from your SAP experience. Score each against the 5 Staff+ signals (Impact, Scope, Depth, Vision, Communication). Goal: You can design systems at L5 level. Your stories are ready but need refinement.

Week 7-8: Advanced LeetCode + Mock Preparation. Solve 50 more Medium problems (total: 130). Cover: advanced DP (DP on trees, DP with bitmask), Sliding Window, Two Pointers, and Interval problems. In parallel, read: Interview Playbook (focus on company-specific differences), Elephant in Room (prepare for objections you will face). Goal: 130+ problems solved, interview strategy clear for your target companies.

Week 9-10: Mock Interviews + Offer Strategy. Take the Readiness Assessment. Do 5 mock interviews (use Mock Simulator and external platforms like Pramp). Refine your stories based on feedback. Read: Offer Negotiation (understand total comp, prepare your target number), SAP Business AI (strategic topic — may come up in behavioral rounds). Goal: Ready for real interviews with confidence.

This path is optimized for SAP engineers with significant experience who are targeting L5/L6 at FAANG. Your advantage: deep enterprise architecture experience. Your disadvantage: LeetCode (you likely have minimal practice) and system design breadth (you know SAP patterns but need generic DS depth).

Week 1: Translation + Strategy. Read: Translation Map (master 20+ pairs — this is your most important resource), FAANG Mindset Shift, Staff+ Technical Framing (understand the 5 Signals — you will need them). Start LeetCode: solve 15 Easy problems in Python to warm up. Goal: By end of Week 1, you can translate any SAP concept to DS equivalents and understand Staff+ evaluation criteria.

Week 2-3: LeetCode Accelerator. Solve 70 Medium problems (focus on: arrays, strings, hash maps, trees, graphs, DP, sliding window, two pointers, binary search). Do not spend more than 45 minutes per problem — if stuck, look at the solution, understand it, and re-solve later. Use Python. At the end of Week 3, you should have 85 total problems solved (15 Easy + 70 Medium). Goal: Solve any LeetCode Medium within 45 minutes with minimal hints.

Week 4-5: System Design Depth. Read: System Design Fundamentals (review), Design Global ERP Platform (your SAP background makes this intuitive — focus on generic DS terms), Design Integration Hub (event-driven patterns), DDIA Replication & Partitioning (consensus, gossip, vector clocks), API Design for Platforms (REST → GraphQL → gRPC). Goal: You can whiteboard any common system design question (design Uber, design YouTube, design a chat system) using generic DS terms while connecting to your SAP experience when relevant.

Week 6: Behavioral + Objection Prep. Read: Interview Playbook (focus on company-specific differences and HC mechanics), Elephant in Room (master all 8 objections — as a Staff- level candidate, you will face more of these). Prepare 5 STAR stories from your SAP career. Score each against the 5 Signals. Practice aloud — record yourself. Fix any SAP jargon. Goal: You have 5 stories that hit 3+ Signals each. You can handle any objection smoothly.

Week 7-8: Mock Interviews + Refinement. Take the Readiness Assessment. Do 5-7 mock interviews (use Mock Simulator, Pramp, and friends at FAANG). After each mock, identify your weakest area and focus on it. Read: Offer Negotiation (know your target — L5/L6 total comp), SAP Business AI (strategic topic for behavioral rounds), Start Here resources (external links for continued learning). Goal: Ready for real interviews with confidence in all areas.

Deliverables</text><text class="se-sub" x="340" y="223">85+ LeetCode, 5 STAR stories (3+ Signals each), system design at L5/L6 level, all 8 objections mastered</text></svg>

This path is for experienced SAP architects and leaders targeting L6/L7 at FAANG. Your advantage: unmatched enterprise experience, deep architecture knowledge, and organizational influence. Your risk: being perceived as too SAP-specific, struggling to adapt to FAANG vocabulary, and facing the strongest objections about 'SAP engineer fit.'

Week 1: Fast Start — Translation + Framing. Read: Translation Map (master all 24 pairs — you must be fluent in both languages), Staff+ Technical Framing (the 5 Signals are your evaluation rubric), FAANG Mindset Shift (vocabulary). LeetCode: solve 10 Easy problems just to verify Python fluency. Goal: By end of Day 3, you can conduct a mock interview entirely in DS terms. By end of Week 1, you understand the Staff+ evaluation criteria and have a translation pair for every SAP concept you will mention.

Week 2: LeetCode + System Design Strategy. Solve 40 Medium problems (arrays, strings, hash maps, trees, graphs, DP — focus on patterns you struggle with). Do not over-invest in LeetCode — as a Staff+ candidate, your coding round is evaluated less strictly than your system design and behavioral rounds. In parallel: review System Design Fundamentals (CAP, consistent hashing, consensus) and identify which topics you need to strengthen. Goal: 50 problems solved, system design review complete.

Week 3-4: System Design Mastery + Behavioral Depth. Read: All 4 system design files (Global ERP, Integration Hub, Master Data, DR). Read: DDIA Replication & Partitioning, API Design for Platforms. Prepare 5-6 STAR stories at Staff+ depth. Each story must demonstrate: org-wide scope (multiple teams influenced), quantified impact ($Ms, % improvements), technical depth (first-principles understanding), and vision (strategic thinking beyond the immediate problem). Score each story against all 5 Signals. Goal: You can design any system at L6 level. Your stories are ready for Staff+ scrutiny.

Week 5: Objection Mastery + Strategic Content. Read: Elephant in Room (master all 8 objections — at Staff+ level, you will face ALL of them), Interview Playbook (HC mechanics, company differences, weak round recovery), SAP Business AI (strategic topic — FAANG is AI-obsessed), Offer Negotiation (your target is L6/L7 — know the comp bands). Practice handling each objection with the structured responses. Record yourself. Goal: No objection surprises you. You can preempt the most damaging ones.

Week 6-8: Mock Interviews + Refinement. Take the Readiness Assessment. Do 5-7 mock interviews. For Staff+ candidates, it is critical to have at least 2 mocks with FAANG Staff+ engineers (use your network, LinkedIn, or paid services like Interviewing.io). After each mock, refine your weakest area. Read: any remaining content you have not covered. Goal: Fully prepared for L6/L7 interviews across all rounds.

details: Staff+ Insight: The path you choose is not a statement about your ability — it is a strategy for efficient preparation. A Senior SAP architect who underinvests in LeetCode because 'I have 15 years of experience' will fail the coding round. A Junior SAP developer who overinvests in system design before understanding CAP theorem will sound shallow. Choose the path that matches your actual gaps, not your ego. The goal is to be ready for FAANG interviews, not to prove you are advanced. Ready? Start with Week 1 of your path.
